rule_text: Freshwater fishing is prohibited in National Parks unless opened under the National Parks Fishing Regulations. Where open, anglers require a National Park Fishing Permit to fish in park waters.

restriction.details: Fishing prohibited unless opened under National Parks Regulations. National Park Fishing Permit required where open.

notes: Applies to all 7 National Parks in BC. Source: Provincial Regulations, 2025-2027 Synopsis, pages 9-10.

rule_text: Fishing is prohibited in Ecological Reserves in B.C.

restriction.details: Fishing prohibited in all Ecological Reserves.

notes: Applies to all Ecological Reserves province-wide. Source: Provincial Regulations, 2025-2027 Synopsis, page 9.

rule_text: Trout: 4 (all species combined).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 4 trout (all species combined).

rule_text: Trout: not more than 1 over 50 cm (2 hatchery steelhead over 50 cm allowed).

restriction.details: Max 1 trout over 50 cm in daily quota (2 hatchery steelhead over 50 cm allowed).

rule_text: Trout from streams: max 2 (must be hatchery origin).

restriction.details: Max 2 trout from streams. Must be hatchery origin.

rule_text: Release all wild steelhead.

restriction.details: Release all wild steelhead.

rule_text: Release all char (includes Dolly Varden).

restriction.details: Release all char including Dolly Varden.

rule_text: Bass: unlimited.

restriction.details: Unlimited daily quota for bass.

rule_text: Crayfish: 25.

restriction.details: Daily quota: 25 crayfish.

rule_text: Kokanee: 5 (none from streams).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 5 kokanee.

rule_text: Kokanee: none from streams.

restriction.details: No kokanee may be kept from streams.

rule_text: White Sturgeon: catch and release only.

restriction.details: Catch and release only for white sturgeon.

rule_text: Yellow perch: unlimited.

restriction.details: Unlimited daily quota for yellow perch.

rule_text: Release of all wild origin trout in streams required. Only hatchery origin trout (identified by healed adipose fin scar) may be harvested from streams. Does not apply to lakes.

restriction.details: All wild trout must be released in streams. Only hatchery trout (adipose fin clipped) may be kept.

rule_text: No fishing in any stream in Management Units 1-1 to 1-6 from July 15 to August 31.

restriction.details: Summer stream closure Jul 15–Aug 31. MUs 1-1 to 1-6.

rule_text: Single barbless hook must be used in all streams of Region 1 and Haida Gwaii (MUs 6-12, 6-13), all year.

restriction.details: Single barbless hook required in all streams, all year.

rule_text: Bait ban applies to all streams of Region 1, all year, with some exceptions noted in the tables.

restriction.details: Bait banned in all streams, all year. See tables for exceptions.

rule_text: Bait ban applies to all streams in Management Units 6-12 and 6-13 (Haida Gwaii), November 1 to April 30.

restriction.details: Bait banned in streams Nov 1–Apr 30.

rule_text: Haida Gwaii trout/char: 5 (all species combined).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 5 trout/char (all species combined).

rule_text: Haida Gwaii trout/char: not more than 1 over 50 cm.

restriction.details: Max 1 trout/char over 50 cm in daily quota.

rule_text: Haida Gwaii trout/char: not more than 3 Dolly Varden.

restriction.details: Max 3 Dolly Varden in daily quota.

rule_text: Haida Gwaii trout/char from streams: max 2.

restriction.details: Max 2 trout/char from streams.

rule_text: Haida Gwaii: release trout/char under 30 cm from streams.

restriction.details: Release trout/char under 30 cm from streams.

rule_text: Haida Gwaii: release all wild steelhead.

restriction.details: Release all wild steelhead.

rule_text: Haida Gwaii kokanee: 10 (none from streams).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 10 kokanee.

rule_text: Haida Gwaii kokanee: none from streams.

restriction.details: No kokanee may be kept from streams.

rule_text: Possession quotas = 2 daily quotas.

restriction.details: Possession limit equals 2 times the daily quota.

rule_text: Annual catch quota for all B.C.: 10 steelhead per licence year (only hatchery steelhead may be retained).

restriction.details: Annual quota: 10 hatchery steelhead province-wide. All wild steelhead must be released.

notes: Source: Region 1 preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. Province-wide rule.

rule_text: Non-tidal salmon fishing regulations are not included in this Synopsis. See DFO regulations at https://www.pac.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/fm-gp/rec/salmon-saumon-eng.html. When fresh waters are closed to fishing or have gear restrictions in this Synopsis, those regulations apply to salmon as well.

restriction.details: Salmon regulations managed by DFO. Stream closures and gear restrictions in this Synopsis also apply to salmon.

notes: Source: Region 1 preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. Province-wide notice.

rule_text: Mercury advisory: Mercury levels in larger Smallmouth Bass in lakes on Vancouver Island and the Gulf Islands may be above national guidelines. The general public, especially children and women of child bearing age, are recommended to limit consumption of Smallmouth Bass.

restriction.details: Mercury advisory for Smallmouth Bass in lakes. Levels increase with fish size. Limit consumption, especially children and women of child bearing age.

rule_text: Single barbless hook must be used in all streams of Region 2, all year.

restriction.details: Single barbless hook required in all streams, all year.

rule_text: Dead fin fish as bait only permitted in Region 2 when sport fishing for sturgeon in the Fraser River, Lower Pitt River (CPR Bridge upstream to Pitt Lake), Lower Harrison River (Fraser River upstream to Harrison Lake).

restriction.details: Dead fin fish bait permitted only for sturgeon fishing.

notes: Source: Region 2 preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. TODO: Needs direct-match IDs to enable.
scope_location: Fraser River, Lower Pitt River, Lower Harrison River

rule_text: Steelhead fishing in the Lower Mainland Region: Your basic licence must be validated with a Conservation Surcharge Stamp if you fish for steelhead anywhere in B.C.

restriction.details: Conservation Surcharge Stamp required for steelhead fishing.

notes: Source: Region 2 preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. Province-wide requirement.

rule_text: When you have caught and retained your daily quota of hatchery steelhead from any water, you must stop fishing that water for the remainder of that day.

restriction.details: Must stop fishing a water for the day once daily hatchery steelhead quota is reached.

rule_text: Protected species in Region 2: Nooksack dace, Salish sucker, Green sturgeon, Cultus Lake sculpin. It is illegal to fish for or catch and retain protected species.

restriction.details: Illegal to fish for or retain: Nooksack dace, Salish sucker, Green sturgeon, Cultus Lake sculpin.

restriction.details: Daily quota: 4 trout/char (all species combined).

restriction.details: Max 1 trout/char over 50 cm in daily quota (2 hatchery steelhead over 50 cm allowed).

rule_text: Trout/char from streams: max 2 (must be hatchery origin).

restriction.details: Max 2 trout/char from streams. Must be hatchery origin.

rule_text: Max 1 char (bull trout, Dolly Varden, or lake trout). None under 60 cm.

restriction.details: Max 1 char (bull trout, Dolly Varden, lake trout). None under 60 cm.

rule_text: Release all wild trout/char from streams. Release hatchery trout/char under 30 cm from streams.

restriction.details: Release all wild trout/char from streams. Release hatchery trout/char under 30 cm from streams.

rule_text: Release all wild steelhead.

restriction.details: Release all wild steelhead.

rule_text: No general minimum size for trout in lakes.

restriction.details: No general minimum size for trout in lakes.

rule_text: Bass: 20 (excluding Mill Lake — see page 24 for quota).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 20 bass. Mill Lake exception — see water-specific tables.

rule_text: Crappie: 20.

restriction.details: Daily quota: 20 crappie.

rule_text: Crayfish: 25.

restriction.details: Daily quota: 25 crayfish.

rule_text: Kokanee: 5 (none from streams).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 5 kokanee. None from streams.

rule_text: Whitefish: 15 (all species combined).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 15 whitefish (all species combined).

rule_text: White Sturgeon: catch and release only.

restriction.details: Catch and release only for white sturgeon.

rule_text: Fraser River: closed to all fishing in the Fraser areas of Jesperson's Side Channel, Herrling Island Side Channel, and Seabird Island north Side Channel, May 15 – July 31.

restriction.details: Jesperson's Side Channel, Herrling Island Side Channel, and Seabird Island north Side Channel closed to all fishing.

notes: Source: Region 2 preamble (under White Sturgeon), 2025-2027 Synopsis. See page 26 for map of closed area. TODO: Needs direct-match IDs to enable.

rule_text: Possession quotas = 2 daily quotas.

restriction.details: Possession limit equals 2 times the daily quota.

rule_text: Annual catch quota for all B.C.: 10 steelhead per licence year (only hatchery steelhead may be retained).

restriction.details: Annual quota: 10 hatchery steelhead province-wide. All wild steelhead must be released.

notes: Source: Region 2 preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. Province-wide rule.

rule_text: Daily and annual quotas for salmon: refer to the notice on page 77 for salmon regulations.

restriction.details: Salmon regulations managed by DFO. See page 77 for details.

rule_text: From one hour after sunset to one hour before sunrise, fishing is prohibited on portions of the Fraser, Harrison, and Pitt Rivers. See water-specific tables for details.

restriction.details: Night fishing prohibited (1 hr after sunset to 1 hr before sunrise). See water-specific tables for details.

notes: Source: Region 2 preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. TODO: Needs direct-match IDs to enable.

rule_text: No fishing in any stream in Region 3 from Jan 1 to June 30 (see tables for exceptions).

rule_text: It is illegal to fish for bass or perch in the Thompson-Nicola Region. This measure is part of B.C.'s management approach to illegal fish introductions.

rule_text: Please report all lake trout caught from Region 4 waters (except Koocanusa Reservoir) to the Kootenay regional office at (250) 489-8540.

rule_text: A permit is required for fishing on all waters within the Creston Valley Wildlife Management Area, including Six Mile, Leach, Kootenay River and Canal and Duck Lake.

restriction.details: A permit is required for fishing in the Creston Valley Wildlife Management Area. Visit www.crestonwildlife.ca or call 250-402-6900.

rule_text: It is illegal to fish for bass, yellow perch, northern pike or walleye in the Kootenay Region. This measure is part of B.C.'s management approach to illegal fish introductions.

restriction.details: Illegal to fish for bass, yellow perch, northern pike, or walleye in Kootenay. Part of B.C.'s illegal fish introduction management.

rule_text: Note: Kootenay Lake fish populations are in a state of recovery. All conservation measures and fishing regulations should be carefully followed.

restriction.details: Kootenay Lake fish populations are in a state of recovery. Follow all conservation measures carefully.

notes: Source: Region 4 preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. TODO: Needs direct-match IDs to enable.

rule_text: Ice Fishing Huts: Failure to remove ice fishing huts from lakes before spring breakup is an offence under the Environmental Management Act.

rule_text: No fishing in all rivers and streams for steelhead, May 15–Jun 15. Exemptions include mainstem portions of the Skeena, Nass, Iskut, Stikine, and Taku Rivers not currently closed under existing winter/spring closure.

rule_text: Set lining is only permitted in the lakes of Region 6. Set lines restricted to one line with a single hook (gap ≥3 cm), marked with angler's name, address and phone. Any game fish caught other than burbot must be released.

restriction.details: Set lines permitted in lakes only. One line, single hook (≥3 cm gap), labelled with name/address/phone. Only burbot may be retained; all other game fish must be released.

rule_text: Trout and Char: 5 (all species combined), including not more than 1 over 50 cm.

restriction.details: Daily quota: 5 trout/char (all species combined).

rule_text: Trout and Char: not more than 1 over 50 cm.

restriction.details: Max 1 trout/char over 50 cm in daily quota.

rule_text: Trout and Char: not more than 2 from streams.

restriction.details: Max 2 trout/char may be kept from streams.

rule_text: Trout and Char: not more than 3 lake trout.

restriction.details: Max 3 lake trout in daily quota.

rule_text: Trout and Char: not more than 1 bull trout (Dolly Varden). May only be retained Oct 16–Aug 14, from lakes only, 30–50 cm in length.

restriction.details: Max 1 bull trout (Dolly Varden). Retention Oct 16–Aug 14 only, from lakes only, 30–50 cm in length.

rule_text: Release bull trout (Dolly Varden) from streams, all year.

restriction.details: All bull trout (Dolly Varden) must be released from streams.

rule_text: Release bull trout (Dolly Varden) from lakes Aug 15–Oct 15.

restriction.details: Release bull trout (Dolly Varden) from lakes Aug 15–Oct 15.

rule_text: Release lake trout of any size Sept 15–Oct 31.

restriction.details: Release lake trout of any size Sept 15–Oct 31.

rule_text: Release lake trout under 30 cm, all year.

restriction.details: Lake trout under 30 cm must be released, all year.

rule_text: Arctic Grayling: catch and release only.

restriction.details: Catch and release only for Arctic grayling.

rule_text: Burbot: 5.

restriction.details: Daily quota: 5 burbot.

rule_text: Kokanee: 10 (none from streams).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 10 kokanee.

rule_text: Kokanee: none from streams.

restriction.details: No kokanee may be kept from streams.

rule_text: White Sturgeon: closed to all fishing.

restriction.details: White sturgeon: closed to all fishing. Endangered under SARA (Upper Fraser/Nechako populations).

rule_text: Whitefish: 15 (all species combined).

restriction.details: Daily quota: 15 whitefish (all species combined).

rule_text: Possession quotas = 2 daily quotas (see tables for exceptions).

restriction.details: Possession limit equals 2 times the daily quota.

rule_text: Lake trout: possession quota = 1 daily quota.

restriction.details: Lake trout possession limit equals 1 daily quota.

rule_text: Bull trout (Dolly Varden): possession quota = 1 daily quota.

restriction.details: Bull trout (Dolly Varden) possession limit equals 1 daily quota.

rule_text: Non-tidal salmon fishing regulations are not included in this Synopsis. See DFO regulations.

restriction.details: Salmon regulations managed by DFO. Stream closures and gear restrictions in this Synopsis also apply to salmon.

notes: Source: Region 7A preamble, 2025-2027 Synopsis. Province-wide notice.

rule_text: The Ministry and partners are conducting tagging studies on various species throughout the Omineca region. Some tags offer cash rewards. Report tag number, colour, date, time and location to Prince George office: (250) 614-7400.

restriction.details: Tagging programs in Omineca Region — some offer cash rewards. Report captures to (250) 614-7400.

rule_text: White Sturgeon in Nechako and Upper Fraser are SARA-listed endangered. Report all sightings or incidental captures to 250-614-7400. See nechakowhitesturgeon.org.

restriction.details: White sturgeon (Upper Fraser/Nechako) are SARA endangered. Report sightings to 250-614-7400. Info: nechakowhitesturgeon.org.

rule_text: Ice Fishing Huts: Failure to remove ice fishing huts from lakes before spring breakup is an offence under the Environmental Management Act.

restriction.details: Ice fishing huts must be removed before spring breakup (Environmental Management Act).

rule_text: Only single barbless hooks may be used in all streams, all year.

restriction.details: Single barbless hooks only in all streams, all year.

rule_text: Bait ban applies to all streams of Zone B, all year.

restriction.details: Bait banned in all streams, all year.

rule_text: Fin fish may not be used as bait in any waters of Zone B.

restriction.details: Fin fish may not be used as bait in any waters.

rule_text: Set lining is not permitted in Zone B.

restriction.details: Set lining is not permitted.

rule_text: Ice fishing huts should have the owner's contact information displayed when left unoccupied. Failure to remove before spring breakup is an offence under the Environmental Management Act.

restriction.details: Ice fishing huts must display owner info and be removed before spring breakup (Environmental Management Act).

rule_text: NOTE: Bull trout and Dolly Varden are two distinct species. Only bull trout are found in the Peace Region.

restriction.details: Only bull trout (not Dolly Varden) are found in the Peace Region.

rule_text: BC Hydro is tracking fish movement throughout the Site C Reservoir, Peace River and tributaries using telemetry. Return tags by emailing tagreturns@golder.com or calling (250) 785-9281. For other areas call (250) 787-3415.

restriction.details: BC Hydro fish tagging study — return tags: tagreturns@golder.com or (250) 785-9281. Other areas: (250) 787-3415.

rule_text: No fishing in any stream in Region 8 from Apr 1–Jun 30. See tables for exceptions.

restriction.details: Streams closed Apr 1–Jun 30 unless noted in tables.

restriction.details: Max 4 trout/char from streams (only 2 over 30 cm).

rule_text: Crayfish trapping: use traps with minimally-sized circular openings to reduce chance of capturing Western Painted Turtles.

restriction.details: Use crayfish traps with minimal circular openings to protect Western Painted Turtles.

rule_text: The Okanagan is the traditional territory of the Syilx people. The Province collaborates with ONA on key initiatives to restore and manage fish stocks.

restriction.details: Okanagan is Syilx traditional territory. Province collaborates with Okanagan Nation Alliance on fish stock restoration.

rule_text: This area overlaps with Indigenous traditional territory. Anglers should be aware that additional rules or access restrictions may apply. Please respect Indigenous rights, cultural practices, and any posted signage.

restriction.details: This area overlaps Indigenous traditional territory. Additional rules or access restrictions may apply.

notes: Advisory — no closure. Always-visible layer for awareness.
